response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong software craftsmanship, TDD, and distributed-systems experience, but the role demands a C++ specialist with 8-12 years of hands-on C++/STL, multi-threaded systems, message brokers, and industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443). Resume shows only a brief 6-month C/C++ stint at National Instruments back in 2014-2015; the bulk of the career is JavaScript/TypeScript, DLang, and Node.js. No evidence of IEC 62443, MQTT, industrial protocols, or secure SDL practice. Mismatch with core technical requirements outweighs the otherwise strong engineering background.
strengths: Broad senior-level experience across distributed, low-latency, and event-driven systems
Strong testing/TDD discipline and mentoring/teaching track record
Familiarity with networked, multi-threaded, high-throughput services (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
concerns: Minimal recent C++/STL experience — only ~6 months a decade ago
No demonstrated IEC 62443, SDL, or industrial-cybersecurity background
No exposure to industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, MQTT) or automation-machinery domain
